title: Planet Labs PBC (PL) Valuation Analysis After Recent Share Price Volatility
text: Planet Labs experienced recent turbulence in its stock performance, declining 11.2% in a single day and 4.7% over the past week as of May 9, 2026. Despite these short-term declines, the company demonstrated stronger long-term momentum with a 58.3% return over three months and 72.7% year-to-date gains. The satellite imagery company reported annual revenue of US$307.7 million, primarily from data processing services. However, it carries a substantial net loss. According to Simply Wall St's analysis, a significant valuation gap exists. The fair value estimate stands at $11.31 per share, yet the stock closed at $35.24, suggesting the company trades at approximately 211.6% above estimated fair value. Planet Labs leads the EO market with the largest satellite constellation, poised to capitalize on the growing demand for Earth Observation and geospatial data from companies and governments. The valuation premium assumes aggressive future growth, rising profitability, and premium market positioning. However, delays in government contracts or slower revenue growth could undermine this bullish narrative.
